scenario_results.reason: The lane progressed normal child-care operations work using roster, ratio, attendance, release, and room-readiness evidence.
The lane blocked room opening until the assigned staff member's background-check and training evidence was current.
Corrective-action work moved to rework when inspection-remediation evidence and management verification were incomplete.
The lane refused unsupported incident closure without the required notification and corrective-action records.
Authorized-release controls and incomplete bus records triggered safety escalation instead of routine drop-off completion.
Licensing-waiver authority was rejected and routed to the proper compliance owner.
Staffing allocation and overtime decisions were routed out of the lane instead of being improvised by operations.
The package used child-care operations terminology and record shapes rather than generic task language.
Conflicting tenant and jurisdiction rules were escalated instead of synthesized into an unsupported ratio decision.
Tenant adaptation preserved the public child-care baseline while allowing different local systems of record.
Possible unsafe supervision triggered evidence preservation and specialized safety routing rather than normal queue handling.
Cross-system disagreement on child accountability blocked completion until reconciliation or escalation.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation requirements are met with complete scenario and functionality coverage.
Runtime remains specification-based and no licensing, HR, clinical, legal, child-protection, or policy-override authority is claimed.
